key_reasoning: The scenario requires two conjunctive conditions, each independently improbable. First, a Trump-Houthi commercial shipping ceasefire has been in place since 6 May 2026 and held as of Day 72; breaking it and producing ≥3 vessel casualties in a coordinated multi-vessel attack within 30 days requires both ceasefire collapse (~35-40% probability) and a rare Houthi mass-casualty operation given their historical pattern of single-vessel targeting (~15-20% conditional probability). Second, and decisively, an Article 5-equivalent response posture from any Western navy is historically unprecedented: during 18+ months of Houthi Red Sea attacks that sank four vessels and disrupted global shipping, Operation Prosperity Guardian and its successors never produced a collective-defense invocation; the UK's HMS Dragon deployment is explicitly framed 'not NATO, not wider war'. The joint probability — ceasefire collapse × mass-casualty attack × Article 5-equivalent declaration — yields roughly 0.38 × 0.18 × 0.04 ≈ 0.003, lifted slightly by the IRGC 30-day deadline running to early June and the 35% B4 scenario weight, producing a point estimate of ~3%.

framework_specific.reference_class: Analogous: 2024 Houthi Red Sea campaign — dozens of attacks, four vessels sunk, zero Article 5-equivalent declarations from any Western navy. Base rate for Article 5-equivalent against non-state maritime actor attacking commercial shipping in post-WWII era: effectively 0.

bayesian_chain.evidence_ceasefire_in_place: LR ~0.3 (strong negative — active ceasefire since 6 May is a real structural barrier)
bayesian_chain.evidence_uk_not_nato_framing: LR ~0.3 (strong negative — explicit institutional pre-commitment against Article 5 framing)
bayesian_chain.evidence_B4_leading_scenario_35pct: LR ~1.4 (weak positive — B4 dominance supports eventual Houthi resumption)
bayesian_chain.evidence_irgc_30d_deadline_june: LR ~1.3 (weak positive — looming IRGC deadline could trigger escalation cascade)

antibiases_applied: Availability: Resisted inflating probability because Houthi attacks are salient in intelligence base — base rate of Article 5 declarations anchors assessment low
Scope sensitivity: Single dramatic attack ≠ Article 5-equivalent posture; most devastating Houthi attack in 2024 did not produce one
Inside view check: Current ceasefire is a real structural barrier, not just a verbal signal — both parties have incentives to maintain it through the Trump-Xi summit window (14-15 May)
framework_specific.what_would_change_mind: A direct Houthi strike on a Western naval vessel with significant crew casualties (not commercial shipping) could push Article 5 probability to 15-20%; complete collapse of Iran MOU talks post-Xi summit could push ceasefire-break probability to 60%, raising joint probability to ~5-7%
